title: Community-submission vetting policy + contributor guide
context: Once the seed library and tooling are established, community contributions from external researchers will be the primary growth path for the protocol corpus. However, allowing unvetted community submissions without a clear policy risks license violations, safety errors, and DURC content entering the library. This unscheduled backlog task defines the community-submission vetting policy and companion contributor guide, gating external submissions through the same quality bar as internal ones while keeping the contribution process accessible.
objective: Design and publish a community-submission vetting policy that gates external protocol submissions through the full license+ethics, safety, and technical review pipeline, and a companion contributor guide that makes the requirements clear and actionable for external contributors.
acceptanceCriteria: Vetting policy specifies: minimum submission requirements (schema-valid, linter-green, license evidence), reviewer assignment for external submissions, and the non-skippable safety sign-off requirement.
Policy explicitly addresses contributor identity (DCO sign-off), conflict-of-interest disclosure, and what happens when a submission fails a gate.
Companion contributor guide is accessible to a non-expert researcher: explains the schema, the licensing requirements, the safety requirements, and how to use the authoring template.
Policy integrated with the contribution workflow (workflow-011) and PR template.
Document committed to the project repo; commit DCO signed-off.
resources: lab-protocols-open workflow-011 (contribution workflow and reviewer rotation)
output: Committed community-submission vetting policy and contributor guide (CONTRIBUTING.md or equivalent), integrated with the PR template and contribution workflow; commit DCO signed-off.
